question_text: Toyota's popular sedan Camry got its name as the anglicized version of the Japanese word kanmuri. What does this word mean?
answer_text: Kanmuri is a type of ceremonial headgear, considered the Japanese equivalent of a crown.
confirmation_text: Yes it is a kind of ceremonial headgear. It can be considered the Japanese equivalent of a crown.

discussion.text: Yes it is a kind of ceremonial headgear. It can be considered the Japanese equivalent of a crown.
Corolla, Corona, Crown - they name a lot of their sedans with crown-like names

question_text: Back in the day FORD had a funny acronym expansion related to reliability. What was that acronym/expansion?
answer_text: Fix or repair daily

question_text: In 1977, the Chauhan Brothers launched this to offset Coca Cola’s exit from India due to government regulations requiring them to reveal their formula and sell 60% equity to a local company. It soon became popular in India until Pepsi launched in 1991 after liberalisation. In 1993, Coca Cola re-entered the market and bought it along with other Parle owned drinks for $60 million. Which famous drink?
answer_text: Thums Up

question_text: X is an Indian legend in her field. However she has also ventured beyond her field with a perfume range -"X Eau de parfum" in 1999. In 2005, she also designed the "Swaranjali" collection of diamond jewellery for an Indian company called Adora. Each piece featured her laser-imprinted signature, and five pieces from the collection were auctioned at Christie's for £105,000, with the proceeds donated to the 2005 Kashmir earthquake relief fund. Id X?
answer_text: Lata Mangeshkar
confirmation_text: Correct 🙌🏻

discussion.text: Diamond voice with a diamond collection

question_text: In 2019, the Norwegian island of Sommaroy made global headlines when locals framed a petition that gained massive attention, though it was never passed. The idea was inspired by the island’s 69 days of continuous daylight, and while the petition itself didn’t succeed, it boosted tourism. It was later revealed that marketing for tourism was the real goal all along. What unusual proposal did this campaign promote?
answer_text: To abolish time and become a "time-free zone" (no clocks).
confirmation_text: Yes, it was to abolish “time”

discussion.text: Awesome! How they survive w/o modern day timekeeping
It appears to have been a publicity stunt
Obviously was a marketing strategy but the concept was the everything including meals will be nature bound and not time bound. People can 2am, food timings based on hunger

question_text: Number 2. Clement Valladingham was a US representative way back in 1863... Vallandigham’s defense for his client [Thomas McGehean] was that Tom Myers had in fact accidentally shot himself while drawing his pistol from a pocket while rising from a kneeling position. Vallandigham managed to save his client in a very unconventional way. The defendant Thomas McGehean, was acquitted and released from custody. How did Clement Valladingham manage to demonstrate his client’s innocence?
answer_text: He accidentally shot himself while demonstrating how the victim might have accidentally shot himself.
confirmation_text: Correct!! He was demonstrating it to his own legal team and ended up getting a bullet in his pelvis

discussion.text: He shot himself while rising from kneeling position?
He tried doing it and shot himself ?
Correct!! He was demonstrating it to his own legal team and ended up getting a bullet in his pelvis

question_text: Number 5. In a recent American football game, Mike Evans received a 55 yard throw from his teammate that resulted in a touchdown. After the touchdown, Mike threw the ball into the stands at Raymond James Stadium. A person in the crowd caught the ball and decided to auction it. In the auction, the football sold for $518,628 through New Jersey auction company Lelands. Unfortunately, within 24 hours of the football being bought, it lost almost it’s entire value, and the sale was voided. What made the football sell for such a high price and why did it lose its value almost instantly?
answer_text: It was thought to be Tom Brady's final touchdown pass before his retirement, but he announced his return to the NFL shortly after.
confirmation_text: Yesss!! Brady pulled a Shahid Afridi and came back from retirement 😶

discussion.text: I think it was supposed to be bradys last pass?
Yesss!! Brady pulled a Shahid Afridi and came back from retirement 😶

question_text: Number 6. This legendary film took almost 11 years in the making with portions being shot, re-shot, edited and so on, as told by the creator (who often abbreviated himself to M.G.S). The plot of the movie goes as follows: The protagonist lives at 451 Hanover Lane, Clarks Summit, PA 18411. He is trying to overcome the demons of losing his wife, when he is summoned by the President of the United States, President Jackson to save the country from his old nemesis. This nemesis is planning on bombing the NHL all star game. ... Released on February 17, 2011 with an IMDB rating of 9.3, which movie are we talking about?
answer_text: Threat Level Midnight (the fictional film by Michael Gary Scott in The Office)

question_text: Number 7. The 1980 film, Cannibal Holocaust tells the story of a group of documentary filmmakers who go missing after they take a trek into the Amazon rainforest to make a movie about cannibal tribes. ... An extremely ‘unique’ promotion strategy for the movie, where most of the cast was absent from the promotions. The combination of a and b however, led to some unintended consequences for director Riggoro Deodata. What were the consequences for the director?
answer_text: He was arrested and charged with murder, as authorities believed the on-screen deaths were real since the actors were contractually obligated to stay out of the public eye for a year.
confirmation_text: Great guess!! . Yes. ↵ The director was charged with murder, with the suspicion that he took method acting/directing too far. ↵ The actors came out of hiding and testified at his trial and he was acquitted.

discussion.text: People thought they were actually dead?

Great guess!! . Yes. ↵ The director was charged with murder, with the suspicion that he took method acting/directing too far. ↵ The actors came out of hiding and testified at his trial and he was acquitted.

question_text: Number 11. In September of 1999, ... the Mars Climate Orbiter burned and broke into pieces... It turned out that there was a miscommunication between the navigation team at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory (JPL) at NASA and the Lockheed Martin Astronautics in Denver, Colorado, which designed and built the spacecraft. What was this miscommunication?
answer_text: A unit conversion error (Lockheed used English units (lb-s), while JPL/NASA used metric (newtons)).
confirmation_text: Yess!! NASA uses metric conversions and Lockheed Martin uses US units.

discussion.text: Yess!! NASA uses metric conversions and Lockheed Martin uses US units.
